---
title: "Contribute"
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Contribute}
  %\VignetteEngine{quarto::html}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
knitr:
  opts_chunk:
    collapse: true
    comment: '#>'
---

## New Tool

Adding a new tool to a {nemo} child package consists of the following steps:

1. Data

Add sample files generated by the tool under `inst/extdata/<tool>/latest/`.
Usually the top 10 rows (plus header) of a representative TSV file are fine.
[DVC] is recommended for tracking the files in version control.
See `vignette("devnotes")` for some DVC notes.

2. Schema

Use `inst/scripts/file_to_yaml.R` to generate a skeleton `schema.yaml` from
the sample files. The script auto-detects raw column names and types; you then
fill in:

- `tidy` — snake_case column name for the tidy output
- `description` — human-readable field description (tool documentation is a
  good source)
- `versions` — explicit array of every tool version the column appears in
  (e.g. `['v1.2.3', 'latest']`); use `['latest']` for columns present only in
  the current version

Save the result as `inst/config/tools/<tool>/schema.yaml`. See
`vignette("structure")` for details on the schema format and how `Config`
reads it.

3. Parsers

Create the R6 class under `R/<Tool>.R`. Use `Tool1` (`R/Tool1.R`) as a
template — it demonstrates the full pattern for `initialize()`, per-table
`parse_*()` / `tidy_*()` methods, and roxygen documentation with
`@testexamples` blocks.

[DVC]: <https://github.com/iterative/dvc> "Data Version Control"
